mapping.a_element: quantum phase transitions in ultracold gases (e.g. the Mott-Hubbard transition in optical lattices)
mapping.b_element: the transition to a supersolid state in the Rydberg-dressed condensate

mapping.a_element: strong/effective interactions driving correlated many-body behavior (Feshbach resonances, on-site Hubbard repulsion)
mapping.b_element: nonlocal isotropic interactions induced by Rydberg dressing

brief_justification: The overlap is only the shared domain (many-body quantum phases of ultracold atomic gases), not shared transferable machinery: paper A is a broad review whose transitions (Mott-Hubbard, lowest-Landau-level, BCS-BEC crossover) are driven by lattice tunneling-vs-repulsion or resonant pairing, whereas paper B's supersolid arises from a roton-maxon softening of the excitation spectrum caused by nonlocal Rydberg-dressed interactions — a distinct mechanism, so no single governing process maps role-for-role between them.
